author: Viktor Ostashevskyi

commands.title: Configure Reasoning Effort for a Model

orcp.models.title: Models
orcp.models.description: Per-model configuration. Keys are OpenRouter model IDs. Set 'enabled' to false to hide a model. Use 'effortLevels' to expose reasoning effort variants (minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh). Set 'cacheControl' to true for models that require explicit prompt-cache markers (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini 2.5+); leave false for providers with automatic caching (DeepSeek, OpenAI). Example: { "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5": { "enabled": true, "cacheControl": true, "effortLevels": ["high"] }, "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro": { "enabled": true, "effortLevels": ["high", "xhigh"] } }. Models not listed are enabled by default with no effort variants and no cache markers.

orcp.defaultEffortLevels.title: Default Reasoning Effort Levels
orcp.defaultEffortLevels.description: Effort-level variants to expose for ALL reasoning-capable models that don't have an explicit 'effortLevels' configured in 'orcp.models'. Example: ["high", "xhigh"] adds those two variants for every reasoning model. Set to [] to disable defaults and only get variants from per-model config.

orcp.providerRouting.title: Provider Routing
orcp.providerRouting.description: OpenRouter provider routing. Example: { "sort": "price" } picks the cheapest provider serving each model; { "only": ["DeepSeek"] } pins requests to the first-party DeepSeek provider. See https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/provider-routing.

orcp.commandSafety.enabled.title: Command Safety: Enabled
orcp.commandSafety.enabled.description: Show an inline 🟢/🟠/🔴 safety verdict above every terminal command the agent proposes. Advisory only — you still approve via the normal confirmation card.

orcp.commandSafety.minLevelToShow.title: Command Safety: Minimum Level To Show
orcp.commandSafety.minLevelToShow.description: Only annotate commands at or above this severity. 'green' annotates everything (incl. read-only); 'orange' shows only caution/unsafe; 'red' shows only unsafe.

orcp.commandSafety.aiEvaluation.title: Command Safety: AI Evaluation
orcp.commandSafety.aiEvaluation.description: Evaluate every command with the model below for a higher-quality verdict. When off (or no model is set), fast built-in local rules are used instead. Allow/block lists always take precedence and skip the model.

orcp.commandSafety.model.title: Command Safety: Model
orcp.commandSafety.model.description: Model (id from this provider) used to evaluate commands when AI evaluation is on. Pick a fast, cheap model — it runs on every terminal command. Reasoning is forced off and the call is time-limited; on timeout/error the local rules are used.

orcp.commandSafety.prompt.title: Command Safety: Evaluation Prompt
orcp.commandSafety.prompt.description: Instruction sent to the model for command evaluation (the command is appended automatically). Leave empty to use the built-in default. Must instruct the model to reply with STRICT JSON {"risk":"green|orange|red","reason":"..."}.

orcp.commandSafety.allowList.title: Command Safety: Allow List
orcp.commandSafety.allowList.description: Command prefixes that are always treated as 🟢 Safe (skips the model). Matched on a word boundary, e.g. "git status" or "npm run". Example: ["npm run", "git status", "ls"].

orcp.commandSafety.denyList.title: Command Safety: Block List
orcp.commandSafety.denyList.description: Command prefixes that are always treated as 🔴 Unsafe (skips the model, takes precedence over the allow list). Matched on a word boundary, e.g. "rm -rf" or "git push --force".

orcp.commandSafety.modalOnRed.title: Command Safety: Modal On Unsafe
orcp.commandSafety.modalOnRed.description: Additionally show a blocking warning dialog for 🔴 unsafe commands. Still advisory — it does not cancel the command.

orcp.commandSafety.redactSecretFiles.title: Command Safety: Redact Secret Files
orcp.commandSafety.redactSecretFiles.description: Withhold the contents of sensitive files (.env, private keys, credentials, etc.) from the model — when the agent reads such a file, its contents are replaced with a placeholder before the request is sent to OpenRouter, so the secret never leaves your device. Covers file-read tools and terminal commands (incl. obfuscated/encoded ones).

orcp.commandSafety.secretFilePatterns.title: Command Safety: Extra Secret File Patterns
orcp.commandSafety.secretFilePatterns.description: Additional file globs treated as secrets (in addition to the built-in defaults like .env, *.pem, id_rsa, .aws/credentials). Examples: ["*.token", "config/secrets.yml", "**/private/*"]. Matched on the basename or full path; .example/.sample/.template files are never treated as secrets.

orcp.commandSafety.redactObfuscatedReads.title: Command Safety: Redact Obfuscated Command Output
orcp.commandSafety.redactObfuscatedReads.description: Aggressive opt-in: when a command (or a script it runs) is obfuscated/encoded (base64, eval, chr()/fromCharCode, octal/hex escapes) such that its target can't be statically resolved, withhold its output from the model and flag it 🔴 — even if no secret file can be named. Prevents leaks via runtime-built paths at the cost of redacting some legitimate obfuscated commands.

description: Access 300+ OpenRouter models in Copilot Chat
name: openrouter-chat-provider

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run clean && npm run package
